product_overview: PreciseHire is an AI-native recruitment orchestration backend that converts Excel applicant lists into scored, role-bucketed candidates and automates pipeline management and recruitment communications. It uses OCR (AWS Textract), NVIDIA NIM for semantic scoring, S3 for storage, DynamoDB for persistence, and AWS SES for email delivery.
core_goals: Automate ingestion of Excel-based candidate lists and reliably persist candidate records.
Provide AI-driven role classification and multi-dimensional ranking against job descriptions using NVIDIA NIM.
Support pipeline orchestration (rounds, status updates) and history tracking for each candidate.
Enable automated, personalized bulk communications (interview invites, assessments, offers) via AWS SES.
Expose health and metrics endpoints for dashboarding and operational monitoring.
Ensure resilience with OCR fallback (Textract), retry/timeout handling, and scalable storage in S3/DynamoDB.

features.name: Upload API
features.description: Accepts Excel (.xlsx) uploads, parses candidate rows, stores the file in S3 and creates candidate records in DynamoDB, returning a jobId for downstream processing.
user_flows: POST /api/upload with multipart/form-data containing valid Excel file (columns: Name, Email, Resume Link) -> Receive 200 { success: true, jobId: "<uuid>", count: <n>, candidates: [ ...previewRows ] } -> GET /api/dashboard/stats -> Receive 200 with updated totalCandidates count including the new jobId
POST /api/upload with multipart/form-data missing required columns -> Receive 400 { success: false, error: "Missing required columns: Email, Resume Link" }
POST /api/upload with a corrupted/unsupported file type -> Receive 415 { success: false, error: "Unsupported file format" }
POST /api/upload with a very large file that times out during parsing -> Receive 500 { success: false, error: "Parsing timeout" }

features.name: Screening API
features.description: Performs AI-powered resume fetching, OCR/text extraction, role-bucket classification, and weighted scoring against a provided Job Description and weightage settings.
user_flows: POST /api/screen with body { jobId: "<uuid>", jobDetails: { title, description, skills }, weightage: { skills: 60, experience: 40 } } -> Receive 200 { success: true, totalCandidates: <n>, roleMatchCount: <m>, candidates: [ { candidateId, role, weighted_score, skills_score, rank } ] } -> GET /api/screen/<jobId> -> Receive 200 { success: true, candidates: [ rankedCandidateList ] }

POST /api/screen with body { jobId: "<uuid>", jobDetails: {...}, weightage: {...} } when resumes include scanned PDFs -> POST triggers OCR fallback (Textract) internally and still returns 200 { success: true, totalCandidates: <n>, roleMatchCount: <m>, candidates: [...] }
POST /api/screen with invalid/missing jobId -> Receive 400 { success: false, error: "jobId is required" }
POST /api/screen with network failure during resume fetch or AI timeout -> Receive 500 { success: false, error: "Resume fetch/AI processing failed for <k> candidates" }

features.name: Email API
features.description: Generates and sends recruitment emails (interview invites, assessments, offers) using AI templates and AWS SES; returns counts of sent and failed deliveries.
user_flows: POST /api/email/interview with body { candidates: [ { candidateId, email } ], details: { jobId, interviewDate, location, customMessage } } -> Receive 200 { success: true, sent: <s>, failed: <f> }
POST /api/email/assessment with body { candidates: [ { candidateId, email } ], details: { assessmentLink } } -> Receive 200 { success: true, sent: <s>, failed: <f> }
POST /api/email/offer with body { candidates: [ { candidateId, email } ], details: { offerDetails } } -> Receive 200 { success: true, sent: <s>, failed: <f> }

POST /api/email/interview with malformed candidate email addresses -> Receive 200 { success: true, sent: <s>, failed: <f> } where failed > 0 and the response contains diagnostic entries for failed recipients
POST /api/email/assessment when AWS SES reports service error -> Receive 500 { success: false, error: "SES service error" }

features.name: Pipeline API
features.description: Creates and manages candidate pipelines (rounds), advances/rejects candidates, and returns pipeline status grouped by round with statistics.
user_flows: POST /api/pipeline/create with body { jobId: "<uuid>", candidateIds: ["c1","c2"] } -> Receive 200 { success: true } -> GET /api/pipeline/status/<jobId> -> Receive 200 { success: true, rounds: ["ATS","Interview","Technical","Offer"], candidatesByRound: { "ATS": [...], "Interview": [...] }, stats: { total: <n>, progressed: <m> } }

POST /api/pipeline/mark-result with body { candidateId: "c1", jobId: "<uuid>", result: "advance", currentRound: "Interview" } -> Receive 200 { success: true } -> GET /api/pipeline/status/<jobId> -> Receive 200 showing the candidate moved to the next round

POST /api/pipeline/mark-result with body { candidateId: "invalid", jobId: "<uuid>", result: "reject", currentRound: "Technical" } -> Receive 400 { success: false, error: "candidateId not found for jobId" }
GET /api/pipeline/status/<jobId> for non-existent jobId -> Receive 404 { success: false, error: "jobId not found" }

features.name: Dashboard API
features.description: Health checks and global recruitment statistics for monitoring and UI dashboards.
user_flows: GET /api/health -> Receive 200 OK indicating API is healthy -> GET /api/dashboard/stats -> Receive 200 with global recruitment stats { totalJobs, totalCandidates, avgProcessingTime }
GET /api/health when a critical downstream service (DynamoDB/S3) is unreachable -> Receive 500 { status: "unhealthy", details: "DynamoDB timeout" }
GET /api/dashboard/stats while background screening jobs are running -> Receive 200 with stats that include inProgressCounts and avgQueueTime
